title: Emergent Ventures
recurrence: rolling, continuous review; decisions typically within weeks
reward: Equity-free grants, typically ~$10k-$50k (some ~$100k); dedicated EV AI tranche for AI projects; repeat grants possible + Unconference community
eligibilityNotes: Verified on official page Jul 2026: 'To be eligible for an EV grant, all applicants must be 13 years of age or older.' Global, any nationality, no student-status or degree requirement; individuals or teams; actively accepting applications (grant amounts not published on page).
fitNotes: Best-fit funding source on the list and explicitly open to minors (13+): Tyler Cowen funds young high-agency outliers in the developing world, and a 17yo Colombian shipping real AI agents/MCP servers is the exact archetype. Apply now — rolling, short application, note AI relevance.

title: Z Fellows
recurrence: multiple cohorts per year; applications reviewed rolling, no deadline
reward: $10k optional investment at a $1B valuation cap (converts at next priced round; program can be done without taking the money) + 1-week intensive with billion-dollar founders + lifetime network; free
eligibilityNotes: Verified on official FAQ Jul 2026: 'there's no age restriction to apply!' Open globally, no company or degree required, can stay in school. Cohorts mostly virtual with the final day in-person in SF or NYC (most fellows attend the full week in person — US visa needed for that).
location: Global (virtual; finale in San Francisco/New York)
fitNotes: Open to minors (no age floor, verified) and optimized for prodigy-type technical builders with shipped side projects — his OSS AI-agent portfolio is the application. Clarify remote participation for the SF/NYC finale and expect guardian help on the $10k paperwork at 17.

title: Latitud Fellowship
recurrence: roughly annual cohorts (LFSF4 is the current one)
nextWindowExpected: LFSF4 verified on official page: opened Jun 13, closes Aug 10, 2026; results Sep 28; program starts Oct 4; SF Week Oct 26-30, 2026
reward: $62.5k ideation check on a SAFE (~2.5%, i.e. $2.5M post-money) + 8-week mostly-virtual program + San Francisco Week (Latitud covers lodging/logistics, not flights); LatAm's flagship -1 to 0 founder program
eligibilityNotes: For LatAm pre-founders at the blank-document (-1 to 0) stage; no co-founder or technical credential required but full-time commitment expected. No age minimum stated, and the official page notes high school students are welcome if they demonstrate impact — signing the SAFE at 17 likely needs a guardian/legal structure, confirm with Latitud before applying.
fitNotes: The application window is LIVE with a verified Aug 10, 2026 deadline, and the page explicitly welcomes high-school-age applicants with impact — so 17 is not an automatic bar. Email Latitud about minor/SAFE mechanics and the full-time expectation vs his degree, then apply this month.

title: Macondo
reward: Hardware project funding up to $1,000 USD per project + shop prizes + free 3-day hackathon in Bogotá Aug 14-16, 2026 + need-based flight stipends; entirely free
eligibilityNotes: Verified on official page Jul 2026: 'You must be a teenager (ages 13-18), and under 19 before August 2026.' No formal application deadline posted — the Aug 14, 2026 date is the verified Bogotá hackathon start, so join the Hack Club Slack and ship funded projects well before it.
location: Bogotá, Colombia (final hackathon); build from anywhere
fitNotes: Open to minors by design (13-18 verified) and it culminates IN Colombia next month while he is still age-eligible — funded hardware, prizes, and an IRL under-18 builder network with zero visa friction. Highest-urgency item given the August date.

title: Merge Grant
recurrence: rolling — average ~10 days from application to funded (verified on page)
reward: $100-$1,000 equity-free microgrant, no strings attached + access to large partner-credits pool (Stripe Atlas, Supabase, Vercel) + young-builder community; short application + relaxed interview
eligibilityNotes: Program page states the Merge Grant is open to all ages and nationalities, aimed at cash-constrained young builders who can't self-fund; recipients across 5 continents. (Sister programs like Magnificent/Afore Grants have their own under-25/under-21 rules.)
fitNotes: Open to minors (all ages verified) and purpose-built for a teenage indie shipper outside the US: fast no-strings cash plus Supabase/Vercel credits that map directly onto his stack. Roughly 10 days from application to funded — send it this week.

title: Platanus Hack — LatAm Tour (Bogotá)
recurrence: annual tour; 2026: Buenos Aires (May), CDMX (Jun), Bogotá Sep 11-13, Caracas (Oct), Santiago (Nov)
nextWindowExpected: Bogotá stop verified for Sep 11-13, 2026; applications marked 'coming soon' on official page as of Jul 10, 2026 (Buenos Aires already opened) — expect ~Jul-Aug 2026
reward: 36-hour zero-to-product hackathon; country winners fly to Chile and the Chile winner to San Francisco (travel paid); direct scouting pipeline into the $200k Platanus accelerator; sponsors include Anthropic, Supabase, Vercel
eligibilityNotes: Verified on page: teams of 3-5, Spanish as primary language; no age requirement published. Organizer materials elsewhere describe a 20-35 target profile and it's an overnight 36h format — a 17-year-old should confirm participation/consent with organizers before the Bogotá stop.
fitNotes: In his country, in his exact stack — the sponsors are the companies whose tools he ships with daily, and it feeds Platanus's accelerator funnel. Age unstated — email organizers to confirm a minor can participate, then recruit his 3-5 person team before applications open.

title: Colombia Tech Week (AI Hackathon)
recurrence: annual (August, Bogotá)
nextWindowExpected: 2026 edition verified on official site: Bogotá, Aug 10-16, 2026; the associated AI hackathon's application/selection details publish on the agenda closer to the date (2025 ran a selective 48h hackathon)
reward: Colombia's biggest founder/investor week (~20,000 attendees) with an application-gated AI hackathon; huge network, content and user-acquisition surface at near-zero cost
eligibilityNotes: Week dates Aug 10-16, 2026 verified on official site; hackathon participation is free by application + selection per prior editions. No published age requirement found — confirm with organizers; being Colombia-based removes all travel/visa friction.
fitNotes: One month away and in-country: the densest week of 2026 for meeting LatAm AI founders and investors, squarely in his skillset, serving his social-media and get-users goals even if he wins nothing. Age unstated — verify hackathon rules for minors before the date.

title: You Ship, We Ship (YSWS)
recurrence: continuous — new ship-to-earn challenges rotate in every few weeks, each with its own reward and timeline
reward: Rotating rewards for shipped projects: hardware grants (historically up to ~$1,000), dev tools, subscriptions, credits, and travel to Hack Club events; verified completely free
eligibilityNotes: Hack Club is for teenagers ages 13-18, so a 17yo university student qualifies for the age-gated programs; the YSWS page itself doesn't restate ages and a few individual challenges specify 'high school' — check each one. Participation via the Hack Club Slack.
fitNotes: Open to minors (teen-native, 13-18) and he already ships constantly — YSWS converts each shipped project into hardware, money, or travel at near-zero marginal effort. This is his last ~year of age eligibility, so harvest it now.

title: Thiel Fellowship
recurrence: rolling applications; ~20-30 fellows/year
reward: $250,000 over 2 years, no equity (verified on official site Jul 2026 — raised from $100k as of the class of 2026), plus the Thiel network of founders/investors/scientists
eligibilityNotes: Official FAQ: aged 22 or younger, no university degree, global, rolling applications; no minimum age stated. Fellows must skip or stop out of college to do the fellowship full-time (current students may apply, then pause/leave to accept).
fitNotes: Age-eligible now as a minor (under-22 cap, no floor) and the check just grew to $250k — but accepting means pausing/leaving his CS degree, so apply once a project shows breakout traction, not before. Ultra-selective.

title: O'Shaughnessy Fellowships
recurrence: annual (2026 window ran Jan 1 - Apr 30, 2026; verified closed)
nextWindowExpected: 2027 window expected ~Jan 1 - Apr 30, 2027, reviewed rolling from day one (estimate from 2026 cycle)
reward: $100k equity-free over 12 months for ~10 fellows + $10k+ OSV Grants for ~20 runners-up; one application considers you for both; fully remote
eligibilityNotes: Open worldwide; proof-of-work over credentials — no degree, company, or pitch-deck requirement. Official page states NO age policy either way (the earlier 'funded fellows aged 17 to 73' claim could not be verified on the page) — email OSV to confirm minors are eligible. 2026 applications closed April 30, 2026 (verified).
fitNotes: The credential-blind 'show your work' ethos suits a public-repo shipper better than any program here, and $100k equity-free is top-tier. Age unstated — verify with OSV before the ~Jan 2027 window (he may be 18 by then anyway); prep materials now and apply day one.

title: Claude Community Ambassadors
recurrence: rolling — applications verified open as of Jul 2026
reward: Event funding/resources covered by Anthropic + monthly API credits + pre-release feature access + product-team sessions + private ambassador Slack + swag (no cash compensation)
eligibilityNotes: Verified on page: global, geographic diversity prioritized, no developer title required, no age restrictions stated; wants people already organizing communities (meetups, Discord/Reddit/X presence) with hands-on Claude Code experience. Anthropic's consumer terms generally require 18+ — confirm the program's age policy before applying.
fitNotes: He builds MCP servers and Claude-based agents daily — becoming the Claude community organizer for Medellín/Bogotá feeds his social-growth, users, and credibility goals with Anthropic footing event costs. Age unstated on the page — verify he can join at 17 before applying.

title: HCB (Hack Club fiscal sponsorship)
reward: Operate under Hack Club's 501(c)(3): a real bank account, ability to legally receive grants/donations/sponsorships, Visa cards, automatic taxes & accounting (verified feature set on page)
eligibilityNotes: Built for teen-led (13-18) Hack Club projects, free for them; other charitable projects pay a fiscal-sponsorship fee. The page doesn't publish age/international specifics — email hcb@hackclub.com to confirm setup for a Colombia-based teen project before relying on it.
fitNotes: Infrastructure, not a prize, and teen-native (open to minors): at 17 in Colombia he can't easily open an account to receive grant money, and HCB is the standard under-18 answer that compounds with every grant on this list (EV, Merge, Macondo).

title: Platanus Acceleration Program
recurrence: batch-based with early/general/late application periods (early applications historically have the highest selection rates)
nextWindowExpected: next batch dates announced on platan.us/newsletter; general application stays open
reward: USD 200k for 7% (post-money SAFE, no fees, wired directly) verified on page + 12-week mostly-online program with in-person kickoff and Demo Day weeks in LatAm cities
eligibilityNotes: Verified on page: Spanish-speaking founders from any country; at least one technical founder who builds; teams of 2-3 preferred (solo possible, lower odds); idea stage OK; requires 100% full-time dedication on program entry. No age requirement published — confirm minor eligibility and SAFE signing with Platanus.
location: LatAm (remote + kickoff/Demo Day weeks in LatAm cities)
fitNotes: The most accessible YC-style $200k in LatAm for a Spanish-speaking technical founder, idea-stage OK and no published age bar (verify at 17). Real constraints: full-time dedication vs his degree and their 2-3 person team preference — the Bogotá hackathon is the natural entry ramp.

title: NEXT (by Makers)
recurrence: rolling cohorts since Oct 2025 launch (8-week program)
nextWindowExpected: next intake announced at nextfellow.ai
reward: 8-week, 100% virtual AI + entrepreneurship intensive for LatAm students aged 13-18: build a real AI-leveraged MVP, pitch investors at Demo Day, join the Makers/Rappi-cofounder network; paid program — 2 merit scholarships funded per paid seat
eligibilityNotes: Explicitly for students aged 13-18 across LatAm (confirmed via official site + Oct 2025 launch coverage); Spanish-language, fully virtual; led by Rappi cofounder Andrés Bilbao. Tuition cost not published — ask about the merit scholarships (2 funded per paid seat).
fitNotes: The rare program where being 17 is the requirement, not the blocker (open to minors by design). Caveat: it's a paid teen bootcamp whose curriculum (no-code, AI tools) is below his level — the value is the Bilbao/Makers network, investor Demo Day, and a scholarship-track stamp, so only do it on scholarship.

title: Masason Foundation
recurrence: annual (2026 recruitment ran Jan 15 - Mar 3, 2026; verified closed)
nextWindowExpected: next recruitment expected ~Jan-Mar 2027 (estimate from 2026 cycle)
reward: No-strings financial support (tuition, research, project/business activities) + member facilities in Tokyo + Son network; ~30 members/year, first year as associate member
eligibilityNotes: Verified on official requirements page: '25 years old or younger at the time of the application', NO minimum age (supports school-age talent). Must meet one of five criteria — his path is criterion 4: preparing/operating a business with demonstrable results. Activities are Tokyo-centric but support is usable from Colombia.
fitNotes: Open to minors (no age floor, verified) and his entry is the business criterion — OSS traction, users, or revenue by January 2027 makes a credible application. Calendar item: window reopens ~Jan 2027.

title: Soma Fellows
recurrence: ~2 batches/year (spring 2026 batch deadline was Apr 1, 2026)
nextWindowExpected: next batch expected H2 2026 (estimate — page shows 'Apply now' with no dates)
reward: Uncapped SAFE advertised as 'up to $2M' (typical checks ~$100k) + fast $10M+ follow-on for strong traction + OpenAI/Anthropic credits + weekly unicorn-founder dinners + SF/NY office access
eligibilityNotes: No age, location, or student-status requirements published (verified page is silent); skews young/student founders and remote participation has been possible, with optional SF/NY offices. Taking a SAFE at 17 needs an incorporated entity and likely guardian involvement — real friction but not a stated bar.
fitNotes: A remote-eligible six-figure SAFE for young builders — worth a shot once one product shows user traction; apply with the strongest product, not the portfolio. Age unstated — verify before applying, and expect minor-paperwork friction on the SAFE.

title: Vercel Open Source Program
recurrence: quarterly cohorts (4x/year)
nextWindowExpected: applications expected to reopen August 2026 (per Vercel community announcement)
reward: $3,600 in Vercel platform credits + third-party starter-pack credits + guidance from Vercel and community, per accepted OSS project
eligibilityNotes: Open to maintainers of active open-source projects globally; judged on the project, not the person; no age requirement published (a Vercel account and its terms are the only personal gate — verify minor account standing).
fitNotes: His MCP-server / AI-agent OSS repos are exactly what this sponsors — free infra plus a Vercel-endorsed badge that compounds credibility. Age unstated but project-judged; low effort, apply when the August window opens.

title: Alianza del Pacífico — Plataforma de Movilidad Estudiantil y Académica
recurrence: annual (recent calls open each April; XVII call ran Apr 1 - May 19, 2026)
nextWindowExpected: XVIII convocatoria expected ~April 2027 (estimate based on the Apr-May 2026 window)
reward: Funded exchange semester at universities in Chile, Mexico or Peru (monthly stipend + support); ~68 undergraduate slots per call across engineering/tech/innovation fields
eligibilityNotes: Colombian citizens enrolled in recognized undergraduate programs apply through ICETEX; requires good academic standing and mid-degree progress (he'll have 4+ semesters by the 2027 call). No explicit minimum age published — he'll likely be 18 by the next call anyway.
fitNotes: The canonical study-abroad route from Colombia for a CS undergrad — Spanish-language, government-backed, realistic for a sophomore timing the April 2027 call (age resolves itself by then). Directly serves the exchange goal with zero founder trade-off.

title: Y Combinator
recurrence: 4 batches/year (Winter, Spring, Summer, Fall)
nextWindowExpected: Fall 2026 on-time deadline July 27, 2026 8pm PT verified on apply page (decisions by Aug 28; late apps still considered); Winter 2027 apps expected ~fall 2026
reward: $500k standard deal ($125k for 7% + $375k uncapped MFN SAFE) + Demo Day and the YC network
eligibilityNotes: Global founders; 3-month in-person batch in San Francisco; no minimum age published on the apply page, but minors face contract (SAFE) and US-visa hurdles; YC's Early Decision path lets students lock in a future batch now.
fitNotes: Age unstated but 3 months in SF at 17 (visa, signing as a minor) makes this cycle a stretch — the smart play is a student Early Decision application to lock a batch for after he turns 18. Deadline for Fall 2026 is Jul 27, verified.

title: Rise
recurrence: annual (applications open ~Sep, close mid-Jan)
reward: Lifetime support for 100 Global Winners/year: need-based 4-year post-secondary scholarship (tuition + living stipend), residential summit, tech package, follow-on project funding, network
eligibilityNotes: Under-18 program: 15-17 from any country. Official portal lists Jan 17, 2027 as the current application deadline. Age cutoff follows a 'as of July 1 of cohort year' pattern (2024 precedent: born Jul 2, 2006 - Jul 1, 2009), implying this cycle requires being 15-17 on Jul 1, 2027.
fitNotes: Open to minors only — but since he's already 17 in July 2026, the Jul 1, 2027 cutoff makes him eligible ONLY if born Jul 2-10, 2009 (a ~9-day band). Check his exact birthdate against the official cutoff the day applications open before investing in this heavy application.

title: Emprendimiento Digital / Apps.co
recurrence: annual convocatorias announced through the year
nextWindowExpected: 2026 components live at convocatorias.innpulsacolombia.com (still open as of Apr 2026 per MinTIC); further calls announced through the year
reward: Free, state-funded specialized mentoring + technical consulting: product-strengthening track (up to 60 teams) and commercial-strategy track (110 digital companies) — support in kind, not cash
eligibilityNotes: Colombian digital entrepreneurial teams (minimum 2 people with a validated conceptual prototype) or constituted companies with revenue; age not specified on public pages, but contracting with state programs typically requires legal age (18) — verify per convocatoria.
fitNotes: The national digital-entrepreneurship pipeline for exactly his shipped-product profile; needs one teammate to qualify. Age likely resolves to 18+ for state contracting — verify per convocatoria or wait for his birthday; modest upside (mentoring, not money) but a legible Colombian-government credential.

title: Emerging Leaders in the Americas Program (ELAP)
recurrence: annual (2026-27 cycle confirmed active; studies begin Aug 2026-Feb 2027)
nextWindowExpected: 2027-28 call expected to open ~Dec 2026-Jan 2027; students are nominated via a Canadian partner university, coordinated by their home university's international office
reward: Scholarship (historically ~CAD 8,200) covering a 4-6 month exchange or research stay at a Canadian post-secondary institution
eligibilityNotes: For students from Latin America/Caribbean (Colombia eligible) enrolled full-time at a home institution; flows through Canadian host institutions' partnership agreements — his university must have a Canadian exchange partner. No explicit minimum age published; exchanges at 17 usually possible with guardian consent.
fitNotes: One of the few funded North-America exchange routes needing no US work authorization or citizenship. Age unstated (likely fine with guardian consent, and he may be 18 by travel); the real action item is institutional — check his university's Canadian partners before the winter nomination round.

title: Claude Campus Program
recurrence: semester cohorts (Fall/Spring)
nextWindowExpected: Fall 2026 applications expected ~Aug-Sep 2026 (estimate; Spring 2026 cohort closed, page says 'stay tuned')
reward: Campus Ambassadors: ~$1,750 stipend for 10 weeks; Builder Clubs: paid stipend + API credits for members + Anthropic speakers/support to run an AI builder club on campus
eligibilityNotes: For currently enrolled university students — he qualifies as a sophomore. Limited to select activated universities and requires eligibility to work in the country of activation (Colombian universities' inclusion unverified; check the school list when Fall 2026 apps open). No age minimum published — a paid stipend at 17 may hit work-eligibility rules.
location: Select university campuses (list varies per cohort)
fitNotes: Founding a Claude Builder Club at his Colombian university would hand him users, an audience, and an Anthropic affiliation in one move — but only if Colombia makes the activation list, and the paid-stipend work-eligibility rule needs an age check at 17. Radar for Aug-Sep.

title: Start-Up Chile (Build / Ignite)
recurrence: 2 application windows/year (BIG 12 ran Apr 2026; BIG 13 expected ~Nov 2026)
nextWindowExpected: official page shows 'Applications opening soon!' as of Jul 2026 — BIG 13 window expected ~Nov 2026 per prior cadence (the previously-reported Nov 30 deadline could not be confirmed on the page)
reward: Equity-free CLP 25M (~USD 26k) Ignite fund + optional CLP 20M extension (verified on page), 4-month program, free coworking, mentors, and a 2-year Chilean startup visa for foreign founders
eligibilityNotes: REQUIRES 18+: official program requirements have stated the team leader must be over 18 at application, hold >=10% equity, and dedicate full-time. Page adds: functional product, early validation, <3 years development. Global applicants welcome; program runs in Santiago.
fitNotes: Requires 18+ — apply after his birthday; the ~Nov 2026 window may line up if he turns 18 before it, so prep the application anyway. High-value equity-free money with visa included, but full-time + Santiago relocation are real costs for a student.

title: Makers Fellowship
recurrence: periodic cohorts (13-week program, 640+ alumni across 8 LatAm countries)
nextWindowExpected: next cohort intake announced on makers.ngo
reward: 100% free 13-week AI-native program (AI Business track: launch an AI startup; Coding track: LLM engineering, multi-agent systems, voice AI) + YC-alumni/Google-circle mentors + internship placements in high-growth LatAm startups
eligibilityNotes: REQUIRES 18+: program materials state fellows are aged 18-24 (Founder track under 25); LatAm-wide, free, selection on potential not credentials. Its teen track NEXT (nextfellow.ai) covers 13-18, which he qualifies for today.
fitNotes: Requires 18+ — apply the first cohort after his 18th birthday (do NEXT meanwhile). Free, the curriculum literally matches his skillset (multi-agent systems, LLM engineering), and it feeds internships at LatAm startups — his goal #2.

title: South Park Commons Founder Fellowship
recurrence: 2 cohorts/year (spring + fall)
nextWindowExpected: Fall 2026 apps close Aug 2, 2026 11:59pm PT (verified on official page); bootcamp late Sep - late Nov 2026
reward: $400k upfront for 7% on a standard SAFE + guaranteed $600k in next external round + up to $1M in partner credits + SPC membership (all verified on page)
eligibilityNotes: For full-time founders at the -1 to 0 stage; no explicit age restriction on page, but fellows must work from SPC offices (San Francisco, NYC, or Bangalore) during the 8-week bootcamp, and the SAFE + full-time commitment imply legal adulthood.
fitNotes: The richest -1-to-0 deal available, but it demands full-time commitment, presence at a US/India office, and signing investment docs — age unstated but realistically an 18+ target once he's ready to go all-in; the Aug 2 deadline this cycle is not for him.

title: a16z speedrun
recurrence: ~2-3 cohorts/year
nextWindowExpected: SR007 apps closed May 17, 2026 (cohort runs Jul 27 - Oct 11, 2026); SR008 applications expected ~fall 2026 (estimate)
reward: Up to $1M investment + up to $7M in partner credits (verified on page); 12-week intensive program in San Francisco
eligibilityNotes: Verified 'open to founders around the world' with visa/banking/relocation support for international founders; program is in-person in San Francisco; acceptance rate under 1%; no published age minimum but investment contracts imply 18+.
fitNotes: AI-agent consumer/dev products are core speedrun territory and the check is enormous, but it's an in-person US program with adult-contract expectations — age unstated, realistically park it for 18+ with strong traction.

title: Interact Fellowship
recurrence: annual (2026 cohort deadline was Feb 9, 2026)
nextWindowExpected: 2027 cohort applications expected ~Dec 2026 - Feb 2027 (estimate from 2026 cycle)
reward: No cash; two fully-sponsored retreats per year + a lifelong, high-signal community of young technologists; free
eligibilityNotes: REQUIRES 18+: must be 18-23 at time of application (rare exceptions for 24); global; free. He qualifies for the 2027 cycle only if he turns 18 before its ~Feb 2027 deadline.
fitNotes: Requires 18+ — a next-cycle application if his 18th birthday lands before the ~Feb 2027 deadline. Zero-cost prestige network of exactly his species of builder; retreats are in the US (visa needed).

title: 500 LatAm Accelerator
recurrence: batch-based; 2026 cadence not published
nextWindowExpected: applications via latam.aplica.500.co (portal live); next batch dates unannounced
reward: $300k investment (verified on page) + Silicon Valley network and strategic support; also runs free 'Release Before Ready' AI hackathons across LatAm as top-of-funnel
eligibilityNotes: Early-stage LatAm founders building globally scalable tech startups; effectively requires an incorporated startup and committed team at investment; no age requirement published (investment contracts imply 18+).
fitNotes: Funds companies, not pre-founders — a 2027+ target once he has a team, incorporation (18+ for contracts), and traction. Their free Release Before Ready hackathons are the piece he can use now at 17.

title: Google for Startups Accelerator: AI First (Latin America)
recurrence: annual cohorts (10-15 startups per cohort)
nextWindowExpected: next LatAm cohort application window announced on the program page
reward: Equity-free 10-12 week accelerator: Google/industry mentorship, Cloud credits, Cloud TPU access eligibility, product credits — no cash investment
eligibilityNotes: Seed to Series A startups based (or with significant operations) in Latin America; deeply technical AI-first products with traction and CTO-level commitment; requires an operating company — not a pre-founder program; no age requirement published.
fitNotes: Perfectly on-thesis (AI-first, LatAm, equity-free) but gated on having a startup with traction, so it's a future milestone, not a current application; age unstated and moot until he has a company. Track for whichever product gets users first.
